report_name: China-Linked SprySOCKS Backdoor Adds Windows Variants with Kernel-Level Stealth

threat_actors.name: FishMonger
threat_actors.description: A China-linked cyber espionage group, part of the Winnti collective, responsible for developing and deploying the SprySOCKS backdoor. They have upgraded the backdoor with Windows variants (WIN_DRV, WIN_PLUS) featuring advanced stealth capabilities, including a kernel-mode rootkit.
threat_actors.goals: Covert, long-term espionage targeting high-value organizations.

threat_actors.name: Winnti Group
threat_actors.description: A broader collective of Chinese state-sponsored threat actors, of which the FishMonger group is a part. Associated with the SprySOCKS backdoor campaign.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor FishMonger historically exploits known N-day vulnerabilities in public-facing applications like Fortinet, GitLab, Microsoft Exchange, and Zimbra for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Scheduled Task
attack_patterns.description: A dropped batch script creates a scheduled task to establish persistence for the SprySOCKS backdoor.

attack_patterns.name: Hijack Execution Flow
attack_patterns.description: The scheduled task triggers a legitimate application vulnerable to DLL side-loading, which then loads the malicious SprySOCKS DLL into a trusted process.

attack_patterns.name: Rootkit
attack_patterns.description: The WIN_DRV variant of SprySOCKS uses the RawWNPF kernel driver, a rootkit component, to hook kernel functions and hide the malware's processes, files, registry keys, and network connections from security tools.

malware.name: SprySOCKS
malware.description: A backdoor attributed to the FishMonger group, previously Linux-only, now with Windows variants (WIN_DRV, WIN_PLUS). The WIN_DRV variant uses a kernel driver for stealth. It is considered an evolution of the Trochilus RAT and shares characteristics with RedLeaves.

malware.name: RawWNPF
malware.description: A custom kernel-mode rootkit driver used by the WIN_DRV variant of the SprySOCKS backdoor. It hooks kernel functions to hide the malware's processes, files, registry keys, and network connections from user-mode applications and security tools.

malware.name: DriverLoader
malware.description: A malware component used by the SprySOCKS backdoor to load the encrypted RawWNPF kernel driver.

malware.name: Trochilus
malware.description: An older Remote Access Trojan (RAT) from which the SprySOCKS backdoor is reported to have evolved.

malware.name: RedLeaves
malware.description: A backdoor that shares characteristics with the SprySOCKS malware.

indicators.name: SprySOCKS Kernel Driver Filenames
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: SprySOCKS Kernel Driver File Creation
id: 2b9c1d8e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files associated with the SprySOCKS backdoor's kernel-level components.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/china-linked-sprysocks-backdoor-expands.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1014
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\RawWNPF.sys'
      - '\DriverLoader.sys'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of kernel driver files associated with the SprySOCKS backdoor.

indicators.name: SprySOCKS Scheduled Task Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: SprySOCKS Scheduled Task Creation for Persistence
id: 8e1d9c2b-5a4f-4c6b-9d8e-e0f1a2b3c4d5
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a scheduled task, a persistence method used by SprySOCKS malware.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/china-linked-sprysocks-backdoor-expands.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1053.005
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\schtasks.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '/create'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of schtasks.exe to create a scheduled task, a technique used by SprySOCKS for persistence.
